title: Task Contract
description: Reliability Harness v2 — Task Contract. Created before any non-trivial change. Captures goal, hard requirements, non-goals, acceptance criteria, allowed scope, preserved invariants, blocking unknowns, and risk tier. Lives in the persistent evidence ledger so context compaction cannot drop it.

task_id.description: Stable unique identifier for the task (e.g., a UUID or slug). Referenced by the verification report.

base_commit.description: Git commit SHA at task start. The clean-checkout verifier creates a fresh worktree from this commit and applies the frozen patch.

goal.description: Short user-intent statement of what the task accomplishes. One or two sentences.

must.description: Hard requirements the task MUST satisfy. Each MUST be mapped to an acceptance criterion and backed by evidence at completion.

must_not.description: Behaviors or outcomes the task MUST NOT introduce (regressions, scope creep, dependency upgrades as side effects, etc.).

non_goals.description: Explicitly out-of-scope items. Calling them out prevents silent scope creep.

acceptance_criteria.description: Machine-verifiable or auditor-verifiable conditions. Each MUST item maps to at least one acceptance criterion.

id.description: Stable ID (e.g., AC1, AC2). Referenced by verification-report.acceptance_results[].

condition.description: Concrete, falsifiable condition. Prefer a command + expected result over prose.

allowed_scope.description: Files / directories the task is permitted to modify. The clean-checkout verifier audits the diff against this list.

preserved_invariants.description: Properties that MUST still hold after the change (e.g., 'API remains backward-compatible', 'no new dependencies', 'public exports unchanged').

blocking_unknowns.description: Open questions that, if unresolved, force STATUS=BLOCKED. Surfaced explicitly so the agent cannot silently paper over them.

risk_tier.description: Drives dynamic routing. trivial=main agent alone; normal=+verifier; complex=2 scouts+lead+verifier; critical=+adversary+test-designer.
